def solve(nums, target)
    n = nums.length

    # 1. Calculate the required XOR sum T' for the removed subset R.
    total_xor_sum = nums.reduce(0, :^)
    t_prime = total_xor_sum ^ target

    # Helper function to generate map: {XOR_sum => min_count}
    def generate_xor_counts(arr)
        counts = {}
        m = arr.length
        
        # Iterate through all 2^m subsets using a bitmask i
        (1 << m).each do |i|
            current_xor = 0
            current_count = 0
            
            (0...m).each do |j|
                if (i >> j) & 1 == 1
                    current_xor ^= arr[j]
                    current_count += 1
                end
            end
            
            # Store the minimum count for this XOR sum
            if !counts.key?(current_xor) || current_count < counts[current_xor]
                counts[current_xor] = current_count
            end
        end
        counts
    end

    # 2. Split nums into L and R
    n_l = n / 2
    l = nums[0...n_l]
    r = nums[n_l...n]
    
    # 3. Generate maps for L and R
    left_map = generate_xor_counts(l)
    right_map = generate_xor_counts(r)
    
    min_removed = Float::INFINITY

    # 4. Combine results using Meet-in-the-Middle
    right_map.each do |xor_r, count_r|
        # We need xor_l ^ xor_r = t_prime
        # So, xor_l = t_prime ^ xor_r
        xor_l_needed = t_prime ^ xor_r
        
        if left_map.key?(xor_l_needed)
            count_l = left_map[xor_l_needed]
            min_removed = [min_removed, count_l + count_r].min
        end
    end

    # 5. Return result
    return min_removed == Float::INFINITY ? -1 : min_removed
end

def solve(nums, target)
    n = nums.length

    # Helper function to generate all possible XOR sums and the maximum size of the subset achieving it
    # Returns a Hash: {xor_sum => max_subset_size}
    def generate_sums(arr)
        s = {}
        n_arr = arr.length
        limit = 1 << n_arr

        (0...limit).each do |m|
            x = 0
            c = 0
            (0...n_arr).each do |i|
                if (m >> i) & 1 == 1
                    x = x ^ arr[i]
                    c += 1
                end
            end
            
            # Update the maximum size found for XOR sum x
            s[x] = [s.fetch(x, -1), c].max
        end
        s
    end

    # Split the array into two halves
    n_a = n / 2
    a = nums[0...n_a]
    b = nums[n_a...n]

    # Generate sum maps for both halves
    s_a = generate_sums(a)
    s_b = generate_sums(b)

    max_size = -1

    # Combine results to find the maximum total size
    s_a.each do |x, c_a|
        # We need y such that x ^ y = target, so y = x ^ target
        y = x ^ target
        
        if s_b.key?(y)
            c_b = s_b[y]
            current_total_size = c_a + c_b
            max_size = [max_size, current_total_size].max
        end
    end

    if max_size == -1
        return -1
    else
        # Minimum removals = Total elements - Maximum remaining elements
        return n - max_size
    end
end

def solve(nums, target)
    n = nums.length

    # Helper function to generate all possible XOR sums and the maximum size of the subset achieving it
    # Returns a Hash: {xor_sum => max_subset_size}
    def generate_sums(arr)
        s = {}
        n_arr = arr.length
        limit = 1 << n_arr

        (0...limit).each do |m|
            x = 0
            c = 0
            (0...n_arr).each do |i|
                if (m >> i) & 1 == 1
                    x = x ^ arr[i]
                    c += 1
                end
            end
            
            # Update the maximum size found for XOR sum x
            s[x] = [s.fetch(x, -1), c].max
        end
        s
    end

    # Split the array into two halves
    n_a = n / 2
    a = nums[0...n_a]
    b = nums[n_a...n]

    # Generate sum maps for both halves
    s_a = generate_sums(a)
    s_b = generate_sums(b)

    max_size = -1

    # Combine results to find the maximum total size
    s_a.each do |x, c_a|
        # We need y such that x ^ y = target, so y = x ^ target
        y = x ^ target
        
        if s_b.key?(y)
            c_b = s_b[y]
            current_total_size = c_a + c_b
            max_size = [max_size, current_total_size].max
        end
    end

    if max_size == -1
        return -1
    else
        # Minimum removals = Total elements - Maximum remaining elements
        return n - max_size
    end
end

def generate_xor_map(arr)
    # Map: XOR_Sum -> Max_Size
    # Initialize with the empty set: XOR sum 0, Size 0
    map = {0 => 0} 
    n = arr.length
    
    # Iterate through all 2^n non-empty subsets using bitmasks
    # 1 << n calculates 2^n
    (1...(1 << n)).each do |i|
        current_xor = 0
        current_size = 0
        
        (0...n).each do |j|
            if (i >> j) & 1 == 1
                current_xor ^= arr[j]
                current_size += 1
            end
        end
        
        # Update the map: only store the largest subset found for this XOR sum
        if map.key?(current_xor)
            map[current_xor] = [map[current_xor], current_size].max
        else
            map[current_xor] = current_size
        end
    end
    
    return map
end

def solve(nums, target)
    n = nums.length
    
    # 1. Split nums into two halves
    n_a = n / 2
    a = nums[0...n_a]
    b = nums[n_a...n]
    
    # 2. Generate XOR sum/size maps for both halves
    map_a = generate_xor_map(a)
    map_b = generate_xor_map(b)
    
    # 3. Combine results to find the maximum possible size (S_max)
    s_max = -1 # Sentinel value indicating no solution found yet
    
    # Iterate over MapA entries (X_A, S_A)
    map_a.each do |xor_a, size_a|
        # We need X_B such that X_A ^ X_B = target, so X_B = target ^ X_A
        xor_b_needed = target ^ xor_a
        
        if map_b.key?(xor_b_needed)
            size_b = map_b[xor_b_needed]
            current_size = size_a + size_b
            s_max = [s_max, current_size].max
        end
    end
    
    # 4. Calculate result
    if s_max == -1
        return -1
    else
        # Minimum removals = Total elements - Maximum remaining elements
        return n - s_max
    end
end

$\text{XOR\_SUM}(S_A) \oplus \text{XOR\_SUM}(S_B) = \text{target}$

Let $X_A = \text{XOR\_SUM}(S_A)$ and $X_B = \text{XOR\_SUM}(S_B)$.
We need $X_A \oplus X_B = \text{target}$, which implies $X_B = X_A \oplus \text{target}$.

We want to maximize $|S_A| + |S_B|$.

**Step 1: Process the first half (A)**
Iterate through all $2^{N_A}$ subsets of $A$. For each subset, calculate its XOR sum ($X_A$) and its size ($|S_A|$).
We need to store, for every possible XOR sum $X_A$, the maximum size $|S_A|$ that achieves it.

Let $M_A$ be a map: $M_A[X] = \text{max\_size}$, where $X$ is a possible XOR sum from $A$.

**Step 2: Process the second half (B)**
Iterate through all $2^{N_B}$ subsets of $B$. For each subset, calculate its XOR sum ($X_B$) and its size ($|S_B|$).

**Step 3: Combine results**
For every pair $(X_B, |S_B|)$ generated from $B$:
1. Calculate the required XOR sum from $A$: $X_A^{\text{needed}} = X_B \oplus \text{target}$.
2. Check if $M_A$ contains $X_A^{\text{needed}}$.
3. If it does, let $K_A = M_A[X_A^{\text{needed}}]$. The total size is $K = K_A + |S_B|$.
4. We update the overall maximum kept size, $K_{\text{max}} = \max(K_{\text{max}}, K)$.

**Step 4: Final result**
The minimum number of removals is $N - K_{\text{max}}$. If no combination was found (i.e., $K_{\text{max}}$ remains at a minimum impossible value, like -1), then the answer is -1.

Let's refine the initialization and tracking. $N$ is the total length.

$K_{\text{max}} = -1$.

### Implementation Details (Ruby)

$N = \text{nums.length}$.
$N_A = N / 2$.
$A = \text{nums}[0...N_A]$.
$B = \text{nums}[N_A...N]$.

**Processing A:**
We can use bitmasks from $0$ to $2^{N_A} - 1$.
$M_A = \{\}$ (Hash map)

For $i$ from $0$ to $2^{N_A} - 1$:
  Calculate $X_A = 0$, $|S_A| = 0$.
  For $j$ from $0$ to $N_A - 1$:
    If the $j$-th bit of $i$ is set:
      $X_A = X_A \oplus A[j]$
      $|S_A| = |S_A| + 1$
  
  If $X_A$ is already in $M_A$, update $M_A[X_A] = \max(M_A[X_A], |S_A|)$.
  Else, set $M_A[X_A] = |S_A|$.

**Processing B and Combining:**
$K_{\text{max}} = -1$.

For $i$ from $0$ to $2^{N_B} - 1$:
  Calculate $X_B = 0$, $|S_B| = 0$.
  For $j$ from $0$ to $N_B - 1$:
    If the $j$-th bit of $i$ is set:
      $X_B = X_B \oplus B[j]$
      $|S_B| = |S_B| + 1$
  
  $X_A^{\text{needed}} = X_B \oplus \text{target}$.
  
  If $M_A$ contains $X_A^{\text{needed}}$:
    $K_A = M_A[X_A^{\text{needed}}]$.
    $K = K_A + |S_B|$.
    $K_{\text{max}} = \max(K_{\text{max}}, K)$.

**Result:**
If $K_{\text{max}} == -1$, return -1.
Otherwise, return $N - K_{\text{max}}$.

Let's structure the Ruby code. We need to handle integer operations and bitwise operations in Ruby.

In Ruby, `**` is exponentiation. `<<` or `>>` for shifting, `&` for bitwise AND, `|` for OR, `^` for XOR.

We will use standard Hash for $M_A$.

One slight optimization/simplification: We are always trying to maximize the size. If $M_A$ already has $X$, and we find a new size $|S_A'| \le M_A[X]$, we don't update.

Let's write the code structure.

__data__.content:
def solve(nums, target)
    n = nums.length
    
    # Split into two halves
    n_a = n / 2
    a = nums[0...n_a]
    b = nums[n_a...n]
    n_b = n - n_a
    
    # M_A stores {xor_sum => max_size} achievable in the first half A
    m_a = {}
    
    # --- Process Half A ---
    # Iterate through all 2^N_A subsets
    (1 << n_a).times do |i|
        xor_sum_a = 0
        size_a = 0
        
        (0...n_a).each do |j|
            if (i >> j) & 1 == 1
                xor_sum_a ^= a[j]
                size_a += 1
            end
        end
        
        # Update M_A: keep the maximum size for any given XOR sum
        m_a[xor_sum_a] = [m_a[xor_sum_a] || -1, size_a].max
    end
    
    k_max = -1
    
    # --- Process Half B and Combine ---
    # Iterate through all 2^N_B subsets
    (1 << n_b).times do |i|
        xor_sum_b = 0
        size_b = 0
        
        (0...n_b).each do |j|
            if (i >> j) & 1 == 1
                xor_sum_b ^= b[j]
                size_b += 1
            end
        end
        
        # We need X_A such that X_A ^ X_B = target => X_A = X_B ^ target
        xor_needed_a = xor_sum_b ^ target
        
        if m_a.key?(xor_needed_a)
            k_a = m_a[xor_needed_a]
            current_k = k_a + size_b
            k_max = [k_max, current_k].max
        end
    end
    
    if k_max == -1
        return -1
    else
        # Minimum removals = Total elements - Maximum elements kept
        return n - k_max
    end
end
